title: China-to-Belize EV Charger Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: Connector Interoperability — GB/T 20234 vs SAE J1772 / CCS1
rows.target_requirement: Belize is a 60 Hz US-heritage Americas market. The applicable practical EV connector baseline is SAE J1772 Type 1 for AC charging and CCS Combo 1 (CCS1) for DC fast charging. IEC 62196 Type 2 / CCS2 should not be assumed for Belize, and GB/T connectors are not physically compatible with the expected vehicle inlets. Connector requirements may be written into a BBS, BEL, site-owner, tender, or charge-point-operator specification, but the engineering starting point for Belize should be J1772 / CCS1.
target_standards: SAE J1772 — AC conductive charging coupler used in North America and Americas 60 Hz markets
CCS Combo 1 / CCS1 — DC fast-charging connector used in North America and Americas 60 Hz markets
IEC 62196-2 — includes Type 1 and Type 2 AC coupler families as dimensional references
IEC 62196-3 — DC and AC/DC vehicle couplers, including CCS configurations
Project-specific BBS, BEL, site-owner, tender, or CPO connector specification
rows.china_equivalent: China AC chargers use GB/T 20234.2 couplers and DC fast chargers use GB/T 20234.3 couplers. GB/T AC and DC connectors are physically incompatible with SAE J1772 Type 1 and CCS1. China DC charging also uses GB/T 27930 CAN communication, which is not the CCS communication stack. A connector swap alone may not be sufficient because control pilot behavior, proximity signaling, locking, cable rating, thermal rise, PLC communication, labels, and test reports may all change.
rows.gap: A China GB/T-only charger is not connector-ready for Belize. Hardware conversion to SAE J1772 Type 1 for AC and CCS1 for DC is required for the US-heritage Americas connector ecosystem unless a specific project states otherwise. Redesign should cover coupler, inlet/outlet geometry, cable assembly, locking, proximity/control pilot signaling, DC communication, temperature sensing, labels, spare parts, and accredited test evidence. GB/T-to-J1772 or GB/T-to-CCS1 adapters should not be treated as a conformity substitute.

mandatory_status.target: Project-critical — SAE J1772 / CCS1 are the applicable practical connector expectations for Belize's 60 Hz US-heritage Americas market; confirm final connector wording with BBS, BEL, tender, owner, or CPO before shipment
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 20234 applies to China domestic connector design and is not a physical substitute for SAE J1772 / CCS1

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] For Belize, use SAE J1772 for AC and CCS1 for DC as the connector baseline. Do not specify IEC Type 2 / CCS2 or GB/T unless the project expressly requires it. GB/T connectors cannot plug into J1772 or CCS1 vehicle inlets.

rows.requirement: BEL Grid Connection — 110/220 V, 60 Hz Revalidation
rows.target_requirement: Belize is a Central American / Caribbean electricity market served by Belize Electricity Limited (BEL) and regulated by the Public Utilities Commission (PUC). The practical supply environment is 110/220 V at 60 Hz, with US-heritage wiring and equipment expectations, and Belize also imports power from Mexico's CFE through the regional supply system. Grid-connected EV charger projects should be coordinated with BEL for service capacity, metering, protection, load management, transformer sizing, and commissioning. For China-made chargers, this is a fundamental electrical mismatch because China domestic equipment is commonly validated for 220 V single-phase / 380 V three-phase at 50 Hz.
target_standards: Belize Electricity Limited (BEL) service connection and project coordination requirements
Public Utilities Commission (PUC) electricity-sector regulatory oversight
Belize 110/220 V, 60 Hz utility supply practice
Mexico CFE import-interconnection context
IEC 61000 series — power quality and electromagnetic compatibility reference
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic charger installations are typically documented against GB/T 18487.1-2023, GB/T 20234 connectors, GB/T 27930-2023 communication for DC charging, and local grid-operator project acceptance. China's common supply baseline is 220 V single-phase / 380 V three-phase, 50 Hz. That baseline does not match Belize's 110/220 V, 60 Hz environment, so input stage, transformer, rectifier, protection, EMC, thermal, and firmware behavior must be revalidated at 60 Hz and the applicable Belize voltage class.
china_standards: China local grid operator project-acceptance requirements
rows.gap: Exporters must confirm: (1) the charger is rated and tested for 60 Hz; (2) AC input supports Belize 110/220 V class service as specified by the site; (3) transformer, rectifier, contactor, RCD/GFCI, surge protection, metering, and enclosure thermal design are valid for Belize supply; (4) EMC and harmonic evidence covers 60 Hz operation; (5) BEL receives service-capacity, load, protection, metering, and commissioning documentation before energization. A China domestic 220/380 V, 50 Hz design is not Belize grid-ready without engineering revalidation.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected installations — BEL site coordination and PUC-regulated electricity service requirements apply; 60 Hz and 110/220 V compatibility must be confirmed for the actual service connection
mandatory_status.cn: China local grid acceptance applies domestically and is not a BEL approval substitute; 50 Hz / 220-380 V evidence is not sufficient for Belize

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Belize readiness starts with 60 Hz and 110/220 V validation. Treat China 220/380 V, 50 Hz grid evidence as a domestic baseline only; prepare BEL-facing service, protection, metering, EMC, and commissioning documentation for the specific site.

rows.requirement: BBS / PUC / BEL Market-Access and Project Scope
rows.target_requirement: The Belize Bureau of Standards (BBS) is the national standards body, the Public Utilities Commission (PUC) regulates public utilities including electricity, and Belize Electricity Limited (BEL) is the national electricity utility. No single official public EVSE whole-unit mandatory certification route was confirmed for all imported EV chargers as of 2026-06-14. Exporters should therefore verify the product HS code, electrical equipment conformity route, labelling, importer obligations, radio module status, and site-specific BEL connection requirements before shipment. For grid-connected installations, BEL project coordination and PUC-regulated electricity service context are separate from product import conformity.
target_standards: Belize Bureau of Standards (BBS) standards and conformity-assessment context
Public Utilities Commission (PUC) electricity utility regulation
Belize Electricity Limited (BEL) service connection and project requirements
Belize Customs and importer HS-code review where applicable
Project-specific tender, site-owner, insurer, or charge-point-operator requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China-market chargers are commonly documented against GB/T 18487.1-2023, GB/T 20234 connector standards, and GB/T 27930 DC communication, with China CCC applying where the product falls within CCC scope. China CCC or GB/T evidence can support engineering review, but it does not establish Belize market access, BBS acceptance, BEL energization approval, or compliance with J1772 / CCS1 and 60 Hz requirements.
china_standards: China CCC (3C) mandatory certification where in scope
rows.gap: Exporters should map the Belize importer, HS code, charger type, rated voltage and frequency, connector configuration, radio modules, labelling, user manuals, safety reports, EMC reports, and BEL project requirements before claiming Belize readiness. China domestic documentation should be converted into a Belize package that explicitly addresses BBS conformity questions, PUC/BEL electricity context, 60 Hz input validation, SAE J1772 / CCS1 hardware, and US-direction safety evidence.

mandatory_status.target: Conditional — exact BBS conformity and import route should be verified for the specific HS code and product; BEL project coordination is mandatory for grid-connected installations; PUC-regulated electricity context applies
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or recommended depending on China CCC scope; GB/T or CCC evidence is not a Belize market-access or BEL energization substitute

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not claim automatic Belize market access from China CCC or GB/T reports. Verify the BBS/import route, PUC/BEL project context, 60 Hz grid fit, SAE J1772 / CCS1 connector design, and safety/EMC evidence for the specific product and site.

rows.requirement: Belize EV Market Context — Nascent Demand and Americas Equipment Fit
rows.target_requirement: Belize's EV charger market should be treated as nascent and project-led. Early deployments are likely to depend on private fleets, tourism sites, hotels, public-sector pilots, and utility or donor-backed infrastructure rather than a mature national charging code. This creates commercial opportunity but increases the need to confirm each tender's technical assumptions. Because Belize uses 60 Hz, US-heritage electrical practice, and regional links to Mexico and Central America, equipment fit should start from Americas voltage, connector, safety, and EMC assumptions: 110/220 V class input, SAE J1772 AC, CCS1 DC, UL-direction safety, and 60 Hz validation.
target_standards: Belize national EV market and infrastructure policy context
BEL grid service and capacity coordination
PUC electricity-sector oversight
SAE J1772 / CCS1 Americas connector ecosystem
UL 2202 / UL 2594 US-direction EVSE safety practice
rows.china_equivalent: China's domestic EV infrastructure market is mature and standardized around GB/T connectors, GB/T 27930 DC communication, 220/380 V at 50 Hz, and Chinese grid-operator practices. Those high-volume domestic assumptions do not transfer automatically to Belize's small, 60 Hz, US-heritage market. Export models intended for Belize should be configured as Americas-market EVSE rather than China GB/T EVSE with paperwork changes.
china_standards: New Energy Vehicle Industry Development Plan 2021–2035 (China)
rows.gap: Belize's market opportunity does not reduce technical validation. Chinese exporters should budget lead time for 60 Hz testing, 110/220 V configuration, SAE J1772 / CCS1 hardware, UL-direction safety review, FCC / IEC EMC evidence, English documentation, corrosion and humidity suitability, and direct confirmation of BEL site requirements before procurement commitments.

mandatory_status.target: Policy and market context — not itself a mandatory product standard; project, BBS, PUC, BEL, importer, and site-owner requirements remain separately applicable
mandatory_status.cn: Policy context only — China EV market scale does not affect Belize conformity or grid obligations

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Belize is an early EV infrastructure market, but the equipment assumptions are not vague: use Americas-market 60 Hz, SAE J1772 / CCS1, and UL-direction safety expectations unless the project states otherwise. GB/T EVSE needs engineering conversion, not just relabelling.

rows.requirement: Networked Chargers, OCPP, EMC and FCC / IEC Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Belize has a nascent EV charging market, so OCPP back-office requirements are likely to be driven by tenders, site owners, charge-point operators, fleets, and payment providers rather than a single confirmed national EVSE platform rule. Networked chargers should still be prepared for OCPP interoperability, SIM / Ethernet routing, remote monitoring, payment integration, firmware updates, and cybersecurity requirements. EMC evidence should address a 60 Hz utility environment; FCC Part 15 practice is relevant for radio or digital devices in US-heritage equipment ecosystems, while IEC 61000 remains a useful international EMC and power-quality reference.
target_standards: OCPP (Open Charge Point Protocol) — back-office communication for networked chargers
FCC Part 15 — radio-frequency devices and unintentional radiators, US-direction EMC practice
IEC 61000 series — electromagnetic compatibility and power quality
Project-specific CPO, fleet, payment, telecom, or site-owner network requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China DC fast chargers commonly use GB/T 27930-2023 CAN communication between the off-board charger and the vehicle BMS. That vehicle-side protocol is separate from OCPP back-office communication and is not the CCS1 communication stack. China domestic EMC and communication evidence may be generated at 50 Hz and with China-market radio modules or operator back offices, so it is not automatically valid for Belize's 60 Hz, J1772 / CCS1, Americas-style deployment.
china_standards: GB/T 27930-2023 — Communication protocols between off-board conductive charger and battery management system
China-market radio module approvals where applicable
rows.gap: Exporters should confirm the required OCPP version, security profile, payment interface, SIM/router bands, remote diagnostics, data hosting, and offline behavior for the Belize project. For EMC, provide conducted and radiated emissions, immunity, surge, harmonic, flicker, and power-quality evidence at 60 Hz, plus FCC Part 15 or accepted equivalent evidence for embedded radio or digital modules where required. GB/T 27930 DC communication does not replace OCPP, CCS1 communication, or Belize project network acceptance.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific — OCPP and network acceptance are likely driven by CPO, fleet, payment, tender, or site-owner requirements; EMC evidence is required where specified by BBS, importer, project, or radio/digital module rules
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 27930 is a China domestic vehicle-side DC charging protocol and is not a substitute for OCPP, CCS1 communication, or FCC / IEC EMC evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Belize networked charger requirements will likely be project-led. Prepare OCPP, CCS1 communication, telecom, and FCC Part 15 / IEC 61000 EMC evidence separately from GB/T 27930, and revalidate at 60 Hz before claiming Belize readiness.

rows.requirement: EVSE Safety Baseline — UL 2202 / UL 2594 and IEC 61851 References
rows.target_requirement: Belize's national standards body is the Belize Bureau of Standards (BBS), while electricity service and utility interfaces are handled through PUC oversight and BEL project coordination. For EVSE in a US-heritage 60 Hz market, safety evidence should be prepared around UL 2594 for AC EVSE and UL 2202 for DC charging equipment where requested by the importer, site owner, insurer, utility, or tender. IEC 61851 can remain a useful international reference for conductive charging safety, but UL-direction evidence is the more practical baseline for a J1772 / CCS1, 60 Hz Americas deployment.
target_standards: Belize Bureau of Standards (BBS) standards and conformity-assessment context
UL 2594 — Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment
UL 2202 — Electric Vehicle Charging System Equipment
IEC 61851-1 — Electric vehicle conductive charging system general requirements
IEC 61851-23 — DC electric vehicle supply equipment
rows.china_equivalent: China's comparable design baseline is GB/T 18487.1-2023 for EV conductive charging system general requirements, combined with GB/T 20234 connector standards and GB/T 27930 DC communication. GB/T 18487 evidence may help explain the domestic design, but it does not prove conformity to UL 2594, UL 2202, SAE J1772, CCS1, or project-specific Belize requirements.
china_standards: GB/T 18487.1-2023 — Electric vehicle conductive charging system — Part 1: General requirements
rows.gap: Exporters should prepare a clause-level safety matrix for UL 2594 or UL 2202 as applicable, 60 Hz leakage-current and protection-device evidence, ground-fault protection evidence, enclosure ingress and corrosion suitability for Belize's humid coastal environment, temperature-rise testing at the Belize voltage class, and installation instructions aligned with BEL site requirements. A standalone GB/T 18487 report is not enough for a Belize-facing J1772 / CCS1 charger package.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific but high practical importance — BBS, importer, insurer, BEL, tender, or site owner may require UL-direction or IEC safety evidence; confirm the accepted route before shipment
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 18487 applies to China domestic design and is not a UL 2594 / UL 2202 substitute

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat GB/T 18487.1-2023 as a China design baseline only. A Belize package should be rebuilt around 60 Hz, SAE J1772 / CCS1, and UL 2594 / UL 2202 or project-accepted IEC 61851 evidence, with BEL installation documentation handled separately.
